data.content_ref: inline:The account claims the collapse was driven by a rapid shift to massed mobile infantry rendering chariot-based armies obsolete, but it excludes Egypt from the scope while noting Egypt survived by adopting these same infantry tactics. This undermines the claimed mechanism: if the military revolution was so swift and decisive that it caused the sudden collapse of all other palatial states, it is implausible that Egypt had time to observe this revolution, adapt its military structure, recruit foreign mercenaries, and successfully counter the threat without experiencing the same catastrophic breakdown. The account provides no explanation for how Egypt alone managed a rapid tactical adaptation that eluded every other major power in the network.
